---
title: "Martin Project Solution"
author: "Jingyi LI"
date: "2026-05-06"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "de_DE.UTF-8")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(knitr)
library(lubridate)

```


```{r}
# 1. Locate all ZIP files in the specified directory using a regex pattern
zip_paths <- list.files("./Projects/martin-raden/PV", pattern = "\\.zip$", full.names = TRUE)

# 2. Iterate through each ZIP file to extract and read the CSV content
pv_list <- lapply(zip_paths, function(path) {
  fname <- unzip(path, list = TRUE)$Name[1]
  read_csv2(unz(path, fname))
})

# 3. Combine all individual data frames into a single master data frame
pv_data <- bind_rows(pv_list)
```

```{r}
pv_data_clean <- pv_data %>%
  # Convert the 'Datum' column from string (DD.MM.YYYY) to a formal Date object
  mutate(Datum = as.Date(Datum, format = "%d.%m.%Y")) %>%
  
  # Batch process all columns containing power metrics ([kWh])
  mutate(across(contains("[kWh]"), ~ {
    as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", gsub("[^0-9,.-]", "", as.character(.x))))
  })) %>%
  
  filter(format(Datum, "%Y") == "2025") %>%
  
  # Handle potential data duplicates for the same day
  group_by(Datum) %>%
  slice_max(`PV Ertrag [kWh]`, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
# Aggregate data by month and calculate financial performance
pv_final <- pv_data_clean %>%
  mutate( `Eigenverbrauch [kWh]` = `PV Ertrag [kWh]` - `Netzeinspeisung [kWh]` - `Speicherung [kWh]`,
  `Gesamtverbrauch [kWh]` = `Eigenverbrauch [kWh]` + `Netzbezug [kWh]`,
  `Eigenverbrauchsanteil [%]` = (`Eigenverbrauch [kWh]` / `Gesamtverbrauch [kWh]`) * 100) %>%
  
  arrange(Datum)

pv_final %>% 
  select(Datum, `Eigenverbrauch [kWh]`, `Gesamtverbrauch [kWh]`, `Eigenverbrauchsanteil [%]`) %>% 
  head()
```

```{r}
monthly_data <- pv_final %>%
  # Extract the month as a number (1-12) and map it to a factor with German labels
  mutate(Month_Num = as.numeric(format(Datum, "%m")),
    Monat = factor(Month_Num, levels = 1:12, labels = c("Jan", "Feb", "Mrz", "Apr", "Mai", "Jun", 
                              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Okt", "Nov", "Dez"))
  ) %>%
  
  # Aggregate electrical metrics by month
  group_by(Monat) %>%
  summarise(across( contains("[kWh]"), 
    ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE) ), .groups = "drop") %>%
  
  # Calculate financial metrics (Costs and Savings)
  mutate(
    `Stromkosten [€]` = `Netzbezug [kWh]` * 0.30,
    `Einspeisevergütung [€]` = `Netzeinspeisung [kWh]` * 0.08,
    `Einsparungen durch Eigenverbrauch [€]` = `Eigenverbrauch [kWh]` * 0.30,
    `Gesamtwirtschaftlichkeit [€]` = `Einsparungen durch Eigenverbrauch [€]` + `Einspeisevergütung [€]` - `Stromkosten [€]`
  ) %>%
  
  # Final formatting
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))

# Output the result as a clean Markdown table for the report
knitr::kable(monthly_data)
```

```{r}
plot_data <- monthly_data %>%
  select(Monat, `Stromkosten [€]`, `Einspeisevergütung [€]`, `Einsparungen durch Eigenverbrauch [€]`) %>%
  mutate(`Stromkosten [€]` = -`Stromkosten [€]`) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Monat, 
    names_to = "Kategorie", 
    values_to = "Euro"
  )
```


```{r}
# 1. Define the color
my_colors <- c(
  "Stromkosten [€]" = "#E67E22",                         # Orange for costs
  "Einspeisevergütung [€]" = "#27AE60",                  # Green for revenue
  "Einsparungen durch Eigenverbrauch [€]" = "#2980B9"    # Blue for savings
)

# 2.visualization
ggplot() +
  # Draw the stacked bar chart for monthly components
  geom_col(data = plot_data, 
           aes(x = Monat, y = Euro, fill = Kategorie), 
           position = "stack", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +
  
  # Add the line chart representing 'Total Economic Benefit' 
  geom_line(data = monthly_data, 
            aes(x = Monat, y = `Gesamtwirtschaftlichkeit [€]`, group = 1), 
            color = "#8E44AD", linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  # Add points on the line for better visualization of monthly data points
  geom_point(data = monthly_data, 
             aes(x = Monat, y = `Gesamtwirtschaftlichkeit [€]`), 
             color = "#8E44AD", fill = "white", size = 3, shape = 21, stroke = 1.5) +
  
  # Apply manual color mapping using the German keys defined above
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  
  # Keep chart titles and labels in the original language as requested
  labs(title = "Monatliche Kosten und Einsparungen 2025",
       subtitle = "Analyse der Wirtschaftlichkeit (Einsparungen vs. Kosten)",
       x = "Monat",
       y = "Euro [€]",
       fill = "Kategorie") +
  
  # Apply a clean, professional theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),           # Remove minor grid lines
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),         # Remove vertical grid lines for clarity
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    legend.position = "bottom"                    # Place legend at the bottom
  ) +
  
  # Add a horizontal dashed line at y=0 as a baseline reference
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey30", linetype = "dashed")
```



```{r}
# 1. Prepare data for the pie chart
pie_plot_data <- tibble(
  Category = c("Einsparung", "Einspeiseverguetung", "Restkosten"),
  Price = c(
    # Use backticks for columns with spaces and units
    sum(pv_data_clean$`Eigenverbrauch [kWh]`, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.30,
    sum(pv_data_clean$`Netzeinspeisung [kWh]`, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.08,
    12000 - (sum(pv_data_clean$`Eigenverbrauch [kWh]`, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.30 + 
             sum(pv_data_clean$`Netzeinspeisung [kWh]`, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.08)
  )
) %>%
  arrange(desc(Category)) %>%
  mutate(
    Percentage = Price / sum(Price) * 100,
    # Labels show only amounts and percentages for clarity
    Label_Text = paste0(round(Price, 0), " €\n(", round(Percentage, 1), "%)"),
    # Calculate exact center position for labels
    pos = cumsum(Price) - Price/2,
    # Calculate angle to determine label direction (left vs right)
    angle = (cumsum(Price) - Price/2) / sum(Price) * 360
  )

# 2. Create the Pie Chart Visualization
ggplot(pie_plot_data, aes(x = 1.8, y = Price, fill = Category)) +
  # Create the ring shape
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "white") + 
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  
  # Use repel to prevent label overlapping and push them to sides
  geom_label_repel(
    aes(y = pos, label = Label_Text),
    size = 3.5,
    # Logic: nudge left if angle > 180, else nudge right
    nudge_x = ifelse(pie_plot_data$angle > 180, -1.2, 1.2), 
    force = 10,
    segment.color = "grey30",
    segment.size = 0.5,
    min.segment.length = 0, # Always draw leader lines
    show.legend = FALSE,
    direction = "both",
    box.padding = 1.2
  ) +
  
  # Manual color scale to fix potential encoding issues with 'ü' in legend
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB"), 
    labels = c("Einsparung", "Einspeisevergütung", "Restkosten")
  ) +
  
  # Clean layout with no axes or grid
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Amortisierung der Photovoltaikanlage",
       fill = "Kategorie") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = unit(c(1,1,1,1), "cm") 
  ) +
  # Expand x-axis to provide physical space for external labels
  xlim(0.5, 3.5)
```
